fixture.description: A panel component loads a job's status asynchronously and writes the resolved result into a field. A refactor removes the request-generation guard, so when select() is called repeatedly in quick succession, a slower EARLIER fetchStatus() can resolve AFTER a newer one and overwrite fresh state with stale data (concurrent async load overwrites newer state). The fix is to restore a per-request id/generation captured before the await and discard the response when it no longer matches the latest request.

grader.rubric: PASS only if the report, in its MAIN body (not an appendix/notes section), flags that JobDetailPanel.select() in src/ui/job-detail-panel.ts writes the awaited fetchStatus() result into this.status without guarding against out-of-order resolution — so when select() is called repeatedly in quick succession, a slower earlier request can resolve after a newer one and overwrite the fresher status with stale data (a stale-async-response / lost-update race) — AND names the correct fix: capture a per-request generation/sequence/request-id before the await and discard (ignore) the response when it no longer matches the latest request (equivalently, restore the latestRequest guard that the diff removed; an AbortController/cancel-previous approach also counts). Severity must be IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the issue is absent, rated below IMPORTANT, surfaced only in an appendix/lower-confidence/dropped-findings section, dismissed as pre-existing, or if the report only complains about the missing field/typing without identifying the out-of-order overwrite. The single-request path is correct and must NOT be called buggy in isolation.

expected_behavior: Bugs/concurrency agent flags that select() can overwrite newer status with an older fetchStatus() response when called repeatedly in quick succession (out-of-order async resolution / lost update)

Recommends restoring a per-request generation/request-id captured before the await and discarding the result when it no longer matches the latest request (or cancelling the prior request via AbortController)
Identifies the bug in src/ui/job-detail-panel.ts at IMPORTANT or CRITICAL severity in the main report
Connects the bug to the removed latestRequest guard shown in the diff
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim a single select() call is buggy in isolation (the issue only manifests under overlapping concurrent calls)
Does NOT dismiss the issue as pre-existing or relegate it to an appendix/low-confidence section
Does NOT rate it below IMPORTANT
Does NOT misdiagnose it as merely a missing null check, a typing issue, or a missing await (fetchStatus IS awaited)
